heritage.title: Culinary heritage
heritage.description: National dishes, their stories, and the nutrition behind each tradition.

hero.title: Sushi in its purest form
hero.description: A calm counter where each piece is shaped in the moment — pristine fish, warm rice, and a rhythm that invites you to slow down.

seasonal.title: Seasonal selection
seasonal.subtitle: A curated taste of what the kitchen is honoring this week.

akami.name: Akami nigiri
akami.description: Lean bluefin tuna with a clean, mineral finish.

uni.name: Hokkaido uni
uni.description: Silky sea urchin over lightly torched nori.

otoro.name: Ōtoro nigiri
otoro.description: Rich tuna belly that melts at the counter.

ebi.name: Ama ebi
ebi.description: Sweet shrimp lightly kissed with flame.

hotate.name: Hotate nigiri
hotate.description: Hokkaido scallop with a whisper of sudachi.

philosophy.title: Precision is our language
philosophy.body: We source in small lots, sharpen our knives before service, and plate with restraint. Every gesture at the counter is meant to keep the fish — not the flourish — at the center.
philosophy.quote: “Silence, steel, and the scent of the sea.”

elena.quote: The pacing felt like a meditation. Each course arrived exactly when we were ready for it.
elena.name: Elena Morales

marcus.quote: Impeccable fish, zero pretense. The omakase told a story from start to finish.
marcus.name: Marcus Chen

yuki.quote: A rare balance of warmth and discipline. We left feeling cared for, not performed at.
yuki.name: Yuki Tanaka

gallery.title: Moments at SHINSEI
gallery.subtitle: A glimpse of the counter, the plate, and the quiet in between.

about.title: About SHINSEI
about.description: An intimate omakase counter devoted to seasonal fish, warm rice, and hospitality that never rushes the meal.

mission.title: Our philosophy
mission.body: SHINSEI exists to serve sushi in its purest form — one piece at a time, shaped for the guest in front of us. Seasonality guides the board; calm attention shapes the room.

approach.title: How we cook
approach.body: We work close to the catch, keep the rice warm, and let precise knife work speak louder than excess. Every seating is a short conversation between chef, fish, and guest.

values.title: What we guard
values.body: Freshness, restraint, and a counter experience that feels personal — never theatrical, never hurried.

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.description: Please read these terms carefully before using the Enterprise landing experience.

acceptance.title: Acceptance
acceptance.body: By accessing this site you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, do not use the service.

use.title: Permitted use
use.body: You may use this site for lawful purposes related to evaluating or adopting Enterprise products. You may not attempt to disrupt, reverse engineer, or misuse the service.

liability.title: Limitation of liability
liability.body: The site is provided as-is without warranties. Enterprise is not liable for indirect or consequential damages arising from use of this site.

changes.title: Changes
changes.body: We may update these terms from time to time. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

blog.title: Blog
blog.description: Architecture notes and product updates from the Enterprise team.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Answers to common questions about the Enterprise landing stack and architecture.

stack.question: What technologies power this landing app?
stack.answer: Next.js 15 App Router, React Server Components, Tailwind CSS, and shared monorepo packages for UI, i18n, and theming.

i18n.question: How is internationalization handled?
i18n.answer: Locale-prefixed routes with server dictionaries for RSC and @enterprise/i18n for client components. RTL layout is handled by the design system.

data.question: How does data fetching work?
data.answer: Server application functions call repositories backed by injectable datasources. Mocks live in _mocks/; the API client handles HTTP only.

performance.question: What performance practices are enforced?
performance.answer: Static generation for marketing pages, minimal client JavaScript, explicit performance budgets, and server-first rendering by default.
